name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on the bench with your feet flat on the floor.
Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar and lower it slowly to your mid-chest.
Push the bar back up powerfully until your arms are fully extended.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to an incline of 30-45 degrees.
Lie back on the bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level.
Press the weights straight up over your chest until arms are locked.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back down to the starting position.

name: Barbell Squat
instructions: Rest the barbell securely on your upper back/traps.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly out.
Lower your body by bending your knees and driving your hips back.
Break parallel, then push through your heels to return to a standing position.

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Grip the pull-up bar with hands wider than shoulder-width apart, palms facing away.
Hang with your arms fully extended (dead hang).
Pull your body up by driving your elbows down until your chin clears the bar.
Lower yourself back down with control to the starting position.

name: Barbell Row
instructions: Hold a barbell with a shoulder-width grip, palms facing down.
Hinge at your hips and bend your knees slightly, keeping your back completely flat.
Pull the bar toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Overhead Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support or stand straight up.
Hold dumbbells at shoulder height with an overhand grip (palms facing forward).
Press the weights directly overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control.

name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
instructions: Stand straight with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended, palms facing forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your torso.
Curl the weights upward by contracting your biceps while keeping upper arms stationary.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the initial position.

name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Attach a rope or straight bar to a high pulley cable station.
Grip the attachment and step back slightly, pinning your elbows to your ribs.
Push the attachment down by extending your elbows until arms are straight.
Squeeze your triceps at the bottom, then return slowly to the starting point.
